causes: alliance system (Triple Entente vs Triple Alliance)
key_battles: Battle of the Somme (60,000 British casualties day 1)
outcome: 15-20 million deaths; collapse of Ottoman, Austro-Hungarian, Russian, German empires; Treaty of Versailles; League of Nations created
legacy: Set conditions for WWII; redrew world map; trench warfare paradigm; chemical weapons first used

key_events: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
outcome: 70-85 million deaths (deadliest conflict in history); UN founded; Cold War begins; decolonization accelerates; Marshall Plan; Nuremberg trials
legacy: Established international law framework, human rights conventions, post-war liberal order

description: Geopolitical tension between US-led Western bloc and Soviet-led Eastern bloc
key_events: Cuban Missile Crisis (1962 — closest to nuclear war)
proxy_wars: Dozens of conflicts worldwide; Korea, Vietnam, Angola, Nicaragua, Afghanistan
nuclear: Mutually Assured Destruction (MAD) doctrine prevented direct superpower conflict; 70,000+ warheads at peak

origin: Great Britain; spread to Europe and North America
economic: GDP per capita began exponential growth after millennia of stagnation; first sustained economic growth in human history
legacy: Foundation of modern capitalism; fossil fuel dependence; global trade networks; technological acceleration

key_events: execution of Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette
legacy: Spread of democracy and nationalism; Napoleonic Wars; end of feudalism; metric system; modern state concept

description: Dismantling of European colonial empires, mostly in Africa and Asia
legacy: Creation of 80+ new nations; Cold War proxy conflicts in newly independent states; economic and political challenges of post-colonial states

description: Cultural and intellectual rebirth beginning in Italy; revival of classical learning
spread: Italy → Northern Europe; printing press accelerated dissemination enormously
legacy: Foundation for Scientific Revolution, Protestant Reformation, Age of Exploration, Enlightenment
